* Applied lots of code quality and performance improvements in multiple phases to the entire code base, guided by the GitHub advanced CodeQL. The details are available on GitHub.

* Improved the logs generated by the Optional Windows Features page's workflows.

* Added a new option to the Sidebar that lets you reboot the system directly to the Recovery Environment (aka WinRE).

* Added secure DoD/DoW certificate installation/removal with validation.

* Reminder: Harden System Security is used by a wide variety of people with different backgrounds, environments, workflows, and security requirements. Because of that, it is completely natural that not every feature will be used by every person. If a new feature is added that you do not need, that is perfectly fine. Unused features remain inactive and do not consume any background resources. New capabilities do not automatically increase CPU or RAM usage. The app is carefully engineered to remain efficient, lightweight, and respectful of system resources while continuing to serve many different use cases. This app is open source and will always remain open source. Its code is publicly available for anyone to review, audit, verify, and contribute to. You do not have to rely on assumptions, guesses, or blind trust. You can inspect the source code yourself, confirm the claims being made, and report any issue you find: https://github.com/HotCakeX/Harden-Windows-Security

* See GitHub releases for full change logs.
